title: Monsters in the Archives by Caroline Bicks review – the writing secrets of Stephen King
description:
A deep dive into the horror novelist’s archives reveals pedantry, penny-pinching, and a total redraft of Carrie

When Caroline Bicks first met Stephen King she was worried. As a teenager she had scared herself silly with his books – Carrie and The Shining were the two that crept under her skin and refused to budge – but now she found herself in the odd position of being Stephen E King professor at the University of Maine. King had endowed the chair at his alma mater in 2016 for the study
hook: Stephen King's archives reveal a writer obsessed with control—every detail pedantic, every draft ruthless, Carrie rewritten from the ground up.

title: Why people are having such strong reactions to Lindy West’s new memoir
description: You might remember feminist writer Lindy West from her days on X (né Twitter) yelling at sexist, anti-fat trolls. Or from her book Shrill. Now, West is back with Adult Braces, a memoir detailing her journey, a literal road trip, to accepting her husband’s request to open up their marriage. Except it wasn’t really a […]
hook: Lindy West's new memoir isn't about being right on the internet anymore—it's about the messier work of staying married while wanting out.

title: Robyn – Sexistential
description:
Robyn

Sexistential

Dumb pleasure is a philosophy on Sexistential, Robyn’s fizzing record of love, sex and motherhood

Sexistential by Robyn

Sixteen years since Body Talk? Eight since Honey? Robyn’s best albums feel closer and further away than that. The Swedish indie popstar’s best music is timeless in that surreal way; untouched b
hook: Robyn's new album treats dumb pleasure like philosophy: love, sex, motherhood fizzing with intelligence and refusal to apologize for joy.
